summary: Offline Device Detection Feature testing complete. All 18 backend API tests passed (100%). Tested heartbeat telemetry, scheduler offline detection logic, L1-only escalation guard, and auto-recovery when heartbeat returns.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/telemetry with metric_type=heartbeat correctly updates device.last_seen and device.status=online
POST /api/telemetry with heartbeat for unknown device correctly returns 404
POST /api/telemetry with heartbeat stores battery_level and signal_strength in metric_value correctly
Heartbeat does NOT create incidents or escalations (verified - no incident_type='heartbeat' incidents created)
SOS/fall_detected telemetry still creates incidents (regression passed)
Scheduler offline detection query correctly identifies stale devices (status=online, last_seen > 10min)
Scheduler creates device_offline incident with type=device_offline, severity=medium when no open one exists
Scheduler idempotency: does NOT create duplicate device_offline incidents for same device
L1-only guard: L2 escalation query excludes device_offline incident_type
L1-only guard: L3 escalation query excludes device_offline incident_type
Auto-recovery: heartbeat from offline device resolves open device_offline incident
Auto-recovery: device status correctly changes from offline to online after heartbeat
Auto-recovery: device_back_online event is logged in incident_events audit trail
seed_data_creation: None - tests use existing devices (DEV-001, DEV-002, TEST-WBAND-827d5e95, TEST-NOTYPE-a0dd1511, E2E-DEV-27596) and create/cleanup test incidents as needed
context_for_next_testing_agent: Offline device detection feature fully functional. Scheduler runs every 60s and detects devices with last_seen > 10min (configurable via device_offline_threshold_minutes). device_offline incidents have severity=medium and stay at L1 only (excluded from L2/L3 queries via L1_ONLY_INCIDENT_TYPES set). Auto-recovery triggers when heartbeat is received from offline device - resolves open device_offline incident and logs device_back_online event. Heartbeat telemetry can include optional battery_level and signal_strength fields which are stored in metric_value.

backend_api_tests: POST /api/telemetry heartbeat updates device to online (201)
POST /api/telemetry heartbeat with battery_level and signal_strength (201)
POST /api/telemetry heartbeat with no optional fields (201)
Scheduler does NOT create duplicate incidents (idempotency)
Heartbeat from offline device resolves device_offline incident
Device status changes from offline to online after heartbeat
device_back_online event logged in incident_events audit trail
Config threshold setting verified (10 min default)

verified_features: POST /api/telemetry with metric_type=heartbeat updates device.last_seen and device.status=online, returns 201
POST /api/telemetry with metric_type=heartbeat for unknown device returns 404
POST /api/telemetry with metric_type=heartbeat with battery_level and signal_strength stores them in metric_value
POST /api/telemetry with metric_type=heartbeat with no optional fields stores empty metric_value
Heartbeat does NOT create incidents or escalations
Scheduler detects stale devices (last_seen > 10min, status=online) and marks them offline
Scheduler creates device_offline incident (type=device_offline, severity=medium) when no open one exists
Scheduler does NOT create duplicate device_offline incidents for same device
device_offline incidents do NOT escalate beyond L1 (L2/L3 queries exclude device_offline type)
Auto-recovery: heartbeat from an offline device resolves open device_offline incident
Auto-recovery: device status goes from offline to online after heartbeat
Auto-recovery: device_back_online event logged in incident audit trail
Config DEVICE_OFFLINE_THRESHOLD_MINUTES is configurable (default 10 min in config.py)
